rulings.Case Title: JULES RADCLIFFE et al VS. PHUONG PHAM et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Compel Defts To Respond To Disovery And To Produce Additional Documents Pursuant To Discovery Requests And Requests For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, December 10, 2014, line 7. PLAINTIFFs JULES RADCLIFFE and MICHAEL JELLISON's Motion To Compel Defendants To Respond To Disovery And To Produce Additional Documents Pursuant To Discovery Requests And Requests For Sanctions DENIED in part and GRANTED in part. Motion is granted as to Requests for Production, 4, 16, 17, 18, 28 as to Pham, LLC. Motion is otherwise denied. = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: JULES RADCLIFFE et al VS. PHUONG PHAM et al
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, December 10, 2014, line 7. DEFENDANTs PHUONG PHAM and TP PHAM, LLC's Motion For Protective Order GRANTED. Requests for financial information of parties and non-parties are overbroad and unduly burdensome. = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: ROBERT BRYAN et al VS. JP MORGAN CHASE BANK, N.A. AS SUCCESSOR IN et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Order To Show Cause Why A Preliminary Injunction Should Not Be Issue
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, December 10, 2014, line 1. Order To Show Cause Why A Preliminary Injunction Should Not Be Issue DENIED, Plaintiff failed to show likelihood of prevailing on the merits. = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: ROBERT BRYAN et al VS. JP MORGAN CHASE BANK, N.A. AS SUCCESSOR IN et al
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, December 10, 2014, line 1. DEFENDANT JP MORGAN CHASE BANK, N.A.'s DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT SUSTAINED with leave to amend as follows: (1) as to causes of action 1-4 for breach of contract and breach of covenant, demurrer is sustained with leave to amend to reconcile allegations in original complaint and in the first amended complaint; (2) as to the 5th cause of action for promissory estoppel, demurrer is sustained with leave to amend to allege facts re: tolling of statute of limitations and to allege all elements of this cause of action; (3) as to the 6th cause of action for violation of Business and Professions Code 17200, demurrer is sustained with leave to amend to allege injury in fact, i.e. loss of money or property not caused by Plaintiff¿s default on the loan. = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: VICTOR TUNG VS. AMI QI et al
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, December 10, 2014, line 8. Plaintiff's Demurrer To X-Complaint Continued to January 8, 2015. Moving party is ordered to comply with SFLR 2.6C and provide courtesy copy of the operative cross-complaint to department 501 no later than December 19, 2014 by 4:00 p.m. = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: THOMAS WONG VS. ALAN ABRUZZO et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Strike Plaintiffs Complaint Pursuant To Ccp Section 425.16
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, December 10, 2014, line 2. DEFENDANTs ALAN ABRUZZO, SARA CORBETT and TIYA-B NUCCI's Motion To Strike Plaintiff's Complaint Pursuant To CCP Section 425.16 DENIED. Moving party failed to sustain burden aND show that Plaintiff's complaint arises out of protected activity. "Terminating a tenancy or removing a property from the rental market are not activities taken in furtherance of the constitutional rights of petition or free speech." Clark v. Mazgani (2009)170 Cal. App. 4th 1281, 1286-87. = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: BENJAMIN SALTHOUSE VS. KAUSHIK DATTANI et al
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, December 10, 2014, line 3. DEFENDANT LAZARO ESCOBAR DEMURRER TO 3RD AMENDED COMPLAINT OVERRULED, pre-existing relationship is not required to assert a cause of action for Negligent Infliction of Emotional Distress. Defendant improperly raises new argument regarding lack of duty in the reply. = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: BENJAMIN SALTHOUSE VS. KAUSHIK DATTANI et al
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, December 10, 2014, line 3. DEFENDANT LAZARO ESCOBAR's MOTION TO STRIKE 3RD AMENDED COMPLAINT GRANTED without leave to amend. Plaintiff failed to allege of assert that the allegations can be made regarding defendant's willful and deliberate failure to avoid/stop the fire. = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: VICTORIANO BUSTO et al VS. ALEJANDRO V. DIESTA JR. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT (Joinder In Alejandro V Diesta)
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, December 10, 2014, line 4. DEFENDANT ALEX DIESTA, JR's MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT (Joinder In Alejandro V Diesta) is DENIED. Triable issue of fact exists as to the meaning of the term "children" as used in the Lease and under the circumstances of this case; and whether landlord improperly withheld consent for occupancy of over 4 adults in a two-bedroom unit. Prevailing party to prepare order in compliance with CRC 3.1312(b). Judge Ronald E. Quidachay, Clerk: M. Olopernes, Not Reported, D501.

rulings.Case Title: VICTORIANO BUSTO et al VS. ALEJANDRO V. DIESTA JR. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT, UNLAWFUL DETAINER
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, December 10, 2014, line 4. DEFENDANTs ROSE DIESTA and ALEJANDRO DIESTA's MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT DENIED. Triable issue of fact exists as to the meaning of the term "children" as used in the Lease and under the circumstances of this case; and whether landlord improperly withheld consent for occupancy of over 4 adults in a two-bedroom unit. = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: MICHAEL J. NILAND VS. HUGO ZEPEDA ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, December 10, 2014, line 5. DEFENDANT HUGO ZEPEDA's DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT OVERRULED, 5 days to answer. Complaint sufficiently alleges facts to support a cause of action for unlawful detainer. Plaintiff may plead in the alternative. Whether or not allegations are true is an issue of fact. Moving party and its counsel are ordered to comply with SFLR 2.6C in future. = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: PGB, LLC VS. MAURICIO ARROYAVE ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, December 10, 2014, line 9. DEFENDANT MAURICIO ARROYAVE's DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT OVERRULED, 5 days to answer. Complaint and Notice sufficiently allege CCP 1161(4) unlawful detainer cause of action. Prayer for costs should have been addressed by a motion to strike. Moving party and its counsel are ordered to comply with SFLR 2.6C in future. = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: 629 POST ST., LLC VS. SANSANEE SAEJEAR ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Defendant'S Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, December 10, 2014, line 6. DEFENDANT SANSANEE SAEJEAR's Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings OFF CALENDAR, untimely per CCP 1005 (filed on December 5, 2014 and set for hearing on December 10, 2014). Additionally, defendant's motion is based on an affirmative defense that is not asserted in the answer. = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: 629 POST ST., LLC VS. SANSANEE SAEJEAR ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Leave To File Amended Answer To Plaintiff'S Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, December 10, 2014, line 6. DEFENDANT SANSANEE SAEJEAR's Motion For Leave To File Amended Answer To Plaintiff's Complaint OFF CALENDAR, untimely per CCP 1005 (filed on December 5, 2014 and set for hearing on December 10, 2014). = (501/REQ)
